title: ATF Morphic resonance field
wikitext:
[]: ATF = Morphic resonance field?             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sat Dec 04, 2010 8:15 pm Does anyone think ATF have some sort of relation to "morphic resonance fields?" "The theory of morphic fields conjectures that all living beings have a field associated with them which defines their entire biology. Morphic fields provide a force that guides the development of an organism as it grows, making it take on a form similar to other species. DNA is not the source of structure itself, but rather a "receiver" that translates instructions in the field into physical form." "The theory of morphic fields and resonance originated as a serious Real World scientific proposal which was pioneered by Rupert Sheldrake PhD. However, most in the scientific community do not accept the idea and regard it as a fringe science or pseudoscience." http://www.sheldrake.org/Articles&Papers/papers/morphic/morphic_intro.html http://www.deepermind.com/08desgin.htm http://tardis.wikia.com/wiki/Morphic_field Despite arguments to the contrary, Evangelion does not use real science, so I think this concept is appropriate.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Sat Dec 04, 2010 9:01 pm More likely comes from Jung's collective unconscious, which is what Sheldrake's bullshit is also based on according to Wikipedia. Plus a healthy dose of Anno yelling, "Everyone who watches this show is a loser with no friends," of course.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 22, 2010 7:30 am Azathoth wrote: More likely comes from Jung's collective unconscious, .
